beats.narration_text: This is Liam for Humanitarians AI.
Privet, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. Shrinking every number from 32 bits to 8 is nearly free — until one tensor quietly loses everything it knew.

props.command: My model quantized fine overall but one layer's accuracy tanked. What causes quantization damage in a specific layer?
output: int8 maps a float range onto 256 bins. Narrow-range tensors collapse many distinct values into the same bin — information destroyed.

beats.narration_text: Here is why Where Quantization Hurts belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

props.command: My model quantized fine overall but one layer's accuracy tanked. What causes quantization damage in a specific layer?
output: int8 maps a float range onto 256 bins. Narrow-range tensors collapse many distinct values into the same bin — information destroyed.

beats.narration_text: From the book: two number lines. Top — weights spread across the full int8 range, clean mapping. Bottom — activations crammed into a tiny range, dozens of float values collapsing onto the same integer bin.

Wide range tensor: values spread from negative 1.5 to positive 1.5. Quantization maps this onto 256 integer bins. Scale factor: about 0.012. Each bin covers about 12 millivolts of range. Distinct float values land in different bins. Quantization error: about 5 millivolts — half a bin. Harmless. Information survives the mapping.

Narrow range tensor: the actual signal lives between 0.001 and 0.01. Same 256 bins, but the range is tiny. Scale: 0.000039. Each bin covers 39 millionths of a unit. Values that were meaningfully different in float-32 collapse into 2, maybe 3 bins. The subtle differences that encode what the layer learned — gone. All mapped to the same integer.

Side by side. Wide range: floats snap to distinct bins across the whole number line. Narrow range: everything collapses to the same two or three bins at the center. The terracotta zone is the mash — where distinct values become identical. One case loses nothing. The other loses everything the layer learned to distinguish.

Your turn. Ask Claude: I'm quantizing a model with these activation ranges by layer — list them. Walk me through: one — how to compute the scale and zero point for each. Two — which layers are at risk of quantization damage based on their range, looking for narrow ranges with informative small-magnitude values. Three — whether per-channel or per-tensor quantization is safer. Four — how QAT would protect the at-risk layers.

props.command: I'm quantizing a model with these activation ranges by layer: [list]. Walk me through: (1) how to compute the scale and zero_point for each, (2) which layers are at risk of quantization damage based on their range (looking for narrow ranges with informative small-magnitude values), (3) whether per-channel or per-tensor quantization is safer, (4) how QAT would protect the at-risk layers.
output: Wide range [-1.5,1.5]: scale≈0.012, error≈0.005 — harmless. Narrow [0.001,0.01]: scale≈0.000039 — values collapse to 2-3 bins.

props.title: Where Quantization Hurts
